Container.Name: unrartool
Container.Overview:
A sleek self-hosted RAR extraction tool built for Unraid. Automatically detects and extracts split RAR archives (RAR5, multi-part .partNN.rar and legacy .r00/.r01 formats) downloaded by Sonarr, Radarr, or any other tool.
Features: - File browser — navigate your media folders and queue RARs manually with one click - Job queue — real-time progress bars and ETA for every extraction - Watch folders — filesystem events trigger extraction the moment a download finishes - Scheduler — periodic background scan of all watch folders - Exclusion system — auto-excludes completed archives so they are never re-extracted; manual Mark as Done button available in the file browser - Force Re-extract — override any exclusion when you need to re-run - Password support — per-folder RAR passwords for encrypted archives - Dark / Light theme toggle - Full log viewer
Container.Description: Automated split-RAR extractor with a modern web UI. Built for Sonarr/Radarr workflows on Unraid.
Container.Changes: ### 1.0.0 - Initial release - File browser with manual queue - Watch folders with filesystem events - Automatic exclusion after extraction - Dark/Light theme - Full log viewer - Password-protected RAR support

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port for the RARUnpacker web interface. Change the left number to remap to a different host port.

Config.Name: Config & Database
Config.Description: Stores the SQLite database, settings, and trash folder. Must be persistent across container restarts.

Config.Name: Downloads / Media Root
Config.Description: Root path that appears in the file browser. Mount your downloads or media folder here. You can add more paths below by clicking Add another Path.

Config.Name: Media Library (optional)
Config.Description: Optional: mount a second location (e.g. your finished media library) so you can browse and manage it from the same UI.

Config.Name: Data Path
Config.Description: The root path shown in the file browser on first load. Should match the container-side mount point of your downloads folder.

Config.Name: Config Path
Config.Description: Where the SQLite database and app settings are stored. Must match the Config volume above.

Config.Name: Web UI Port (env)
Config.Description: Internal port the web server listens on. Must match the Port mapping above.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID the container runs as. Set to your Unraid user ID (usually 99 for nobody) to ensure extracted files are owned correctly.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID the container runs as. Set to your Unraid group ID (usually 100 for users).
